  • Module
  • Package

  • Help

All Classes and Interfaces

Classes
Class
Description
AddAppointmentForm
controls my add appointment/update appointment forms
AddCustomerForm
controls my add customer form (which is also the update form :) )
Appointment
describes Appointment objects and their methods
AppointmentDAO
handles database access and operations dealing with appointments
Contact
describes contact object and its methods
ContactDAO
handles database access and operations for contact objects
Country
Represents a country with an ID and name.
CountryDAO
handles database access and operations involving countries
Customer
describes customer objects and its methods
CustomerDAO
handles database access and operations dealing with customer objects
Division
Represents a first-level division (e.g., state, province) with an ID, name, and associated country ID.
DivisionDAO
handles database access and operations involving first-level division objects
FormLoader
helper class to open forms, allowing for cleaner code in the rest of the project
JDBC
This class manages the database connection to a MySQL database.
LoginForm
controls the behavior of my login form
Main
The main entry point of the Appointment Scheduling System application.
MainMenuForm
controls the main menu form
MonthMap
The MonthMap class provides functionality to map numeric month indices (1 through 12) to their localized names based on the current application locale.
Session
holds session-level data such as currently logged-in user, that way it's simpler to retrieve, and we don't have to actually find it multiple times
User
Represents a user with a unique ID, username, and password.
UserDAO
handles database access and operations involving user objects
ViewAppointments
controls the View appointments form
ViewCustomers
governs the ViewCustomers screen
ViewReports
The ViewReports controller handles the logic for generating and displaying various reports.
Wrapper
A simple wrapper class to allow building an executable JAR with Maven and JavaFX.
